title: Code Review Standards
content:
## Code Review Guidelines

### Before Submitting
- Self-review your changes first
- Ensure all tests pass locally
- Keep PRs under 400 lines when possible
- Write a clear PR description

### Review Checklist
- [ ] Logic is correct and handles edge cases
- [ ] No security vulnerabilities introduced
- [ ] Code follows team conventions
- [ ] Tests cover the changes
- [ ] No unnecessary complexity added

### Giving Feedback
- Be kind, be specific, be constructive
- Prefix comments: `nit:`, `question:`, `suggestion:`, `blocker:`
- Approve when comfortable, don't block on nits

title: Naming Conventions
content:
## Naming Conventions

### Files
- Components: `kebab-case.ts` (e.g., `user-profile.ts`)
- Services: `kebab-case.service.ts`
- Models: `kebab-case.model.ts`
- Tests: `kebab-case.spec.ts`

### TypeScript
- Classes/Interfaces: `PascalCase`
- Functions/Methods: `camelCase`
- Constants: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`
- Private members: no underscore prefix

### Angular Signals
- Signals: `camelCase` (e.g., `userName`)
- Computed: descriptive name (e.g., `fullName`)
- Effects: verb-based (e.g., `syncToLocalStorage`)

title: Git Workflow
content:
## Git Workflow

### Branch Naming
```
feature/TICKET-123-short-description
fix/TICKET-456-bug-description
chore/update-dependencies
```

### Commit Messages
Follow conventional commits:
```
feat: add user authentication
fix: resolve login redirect loop
chore: update Angular to v21
docs: add API documentation
test: add unit tests for auth service
```

### PR Flow
1. Create feature branch from `main`
2. Make small, focused commits
3. Push and create PR
4. Get at least 1 approval
5. Squash merge to `main`

title: Testing Strategy
content:
## Testing Strategy

### Test Pyramid
- **Unit Tests (Vitest)**: 70% — Components, services, pipes
- **Integration Tests**: 20% — Feature modules, service interactions
- **E2E Tests (Playwright)**: 10% — Critical user journeys

### What to Test
- Business logic and state management
- Component inputs/outputs and user interactions
- Error handling and edge cases
- Accessibility (keyboard nav, ARIA)

### What NOT to Test
- Framework internals
- Third-party library behavior
- Pure HTML/CSS rendering
- Private methods directly

title: Accessibility Standards
content:
## Accessibility Standards (WCAG 2.1 AA)

### Required
- All interactive elements must be keyboard accessible
- Color contrast ratio: minimum 4.5:1 for text
- All images must have alt text
- Focus indicators must be visible
- Form fields need associated labels

### ARIA Usage
- Use semantic HTML first (`nav`, `main`, `section`)
- Add ARIA only when HTML semantics aren't sufficient
- `aria-label` for icon-only buttons
- `aria-live` for dynamic content updates
- `role="status"` for toast notifications

### Testing
- Use axe-core in CI pipeline
- Manual keyboard navigation testing
- Screen reader testing (NVDA/VoiceOver)
